title: The shop that opens at four
summary: Mrs Lan opens her small bread shop at 4 a.m. — before the city wakes — for the people who go to work before anyone else.
body:
Every morning at four, before the city wakes up, Mrs Lan opens her bread shop. She has done this for twenty-three years. Her shop is small. There are only three tables and a window.

Why does she open so early? She says, 'Bus drivers work at five. Nurses work at six. Cleaners work at seven. They are my customers.'

For most of her life, Mrs Lan made the same three things. Bread with sweet butter. Bread with salty meat. Tea, very hot, in small cups.

She has changed only one thing. Last year, she put a small notebook on the corner table. It is a free notebook. Anyone can write in it. Some customers write their thoughts. Some draw a picture. One man wrote a recipe for cake. Another wrote a poem about his late wife.

Mrs Lan reads the notebook on Sundays. She does not respond. She does not show it to anyone. But she keeps every page.

A young woman once asked her, 'Why a notebook?'

Mrs Lan said, 'My customers leave home before the sun. They go to work before the city is awake. Most days, no one talks to them until they get to their job. The notebook listens.'

Mrs Lan is sixty-eight. She will close her shop one day. She has not said when. The notebooks — there are now nine — will go with her.

annotations.meaningVi: thức dậy (cụm động từ: wake up)
cái bàn ở góc (phòng / cửa hàng)
công thức nấu ăn / làm bánh
rời nhà trước khi mặt trời mọc (biểu đạt việc đi làm rất sớm)
thành phố đã thức (cụm diễn đạt chỉ thời điểm ngày mới bắt đầu)
hầu hết các ngày, thường thì
bơ ngọt (bơ không muối dùng trong ẩm thực)
thịt mặn (collocation: thịt ướp muối dùng trong bánh mì)
Cuốn sổ lắng nghe — nhân hóa vật thể, ý nói sổ thay thế sự kết nối người với người
Người quét dọn / lao công (nghề nghiệp)

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect with 'for + period'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại hoàn thành (have/has + V3) với 'for + khoảng thời gian' để nói về một hành động bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn tiếp tục đến hiện tại. Trong bài: 'She has done this for twenty-three years.' (Bà đã làm việc này được hai mươi ba năm.) / 'She has changed only one thing.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present perfect with 'for + period' for an action that started in the past and continues now. From the story: 'She has done this for twenty-three years.' / 'She has changed only one thing.' / 'She has not said when.'

cloze.template:
Every morning at {{b1}}, before the city wakes up, Mrs Lan opens her bread shop. She has done this for {{b2}} years. Her shop is small. There are only three tables and a window.

Why does she open so early? She says, '{{b3}} drivers work at five. {{b4}} work at six. Cleaners work at seven. They are my customers.'

For most of her life, Mrs Lan made the same three things. Bread with sweet {{b5}}. Bread with salty meat. Tea, very hot, in small cups.

She has changed only one thing. Last year, she put a small {{b6}} on the corner table. It is a free notebook. Anyone can write in it. Some customers write their thoughts. Some draw a picture. One man wrote a {{b7}} for cake. Another wrote a {{b8}} about his late wife.

Mrs Lan reads the notebook on {{b9}}. She does not respond. She does not show it to anyone. But she keeps every page.

A young woman once asked her, 'Why a notebook?'

Mrs Lan said, 'My customers leave home before the sun. They go to work before the city is awake. Most days, no one talks to them until they get to their job. The notebook {{b10}}.'

Mrs Lan is {{b11}}. She will close her shop one day. She has not said when. The notebooks — there are now {{b12}} — will go with her.

blanks.explanation: She opens at 4 a.m., before the city wakes.
She has done this for twenty-three years.
Bus drivers start work at five — her earliest customers.
Nurses work at six — her second wave of customers.
Bread with sweet butter — one of the three things she has always made.
She placed a small notebook on the corner table.
One man wrote a recipe for cake.
Another wrote a poem about his late wife.
Mrs Lan reads the notebook on Sundays.
'The notebook listens.' — Mrs Lan's quiet explanation.
There are now nine notebooks — one per year since she started.

translationVi:
Mỗi sáng lúc bốn giờ, trước khi cả thành phố thức dậy, bà Lan mở cửa hàng bánh mì của mình. Bà đã làm việc này được hai mươi ba năm. Cửa hàng bà nhỏ. Chỉ có ba cái bàn và một ô cửa sổ.

Vì sao bà mở sớm thế? Bà nói: 'Tài xế xe buýt làm việc lúc năm giờ. Y tá làm việc lúc sáu giờ. Người quét đường làm việc lúc bảy giờ. Họ là khách hàng của tôi.'

Gần như suốt cuộc đời, bà Lan chỉ làm cùng ba thứ. Bánh mì với bơ ngọt. Bánh mì với thịt mặn. Trà, thật nóng, trong những cái cốc nhỏ.

Bà chỉ thay đổi một điều. Năm ngoái, bà đặt một cuốn sổ nhỏ trên cái bàn ở góc. Đó là cuốn sổ miễn phí. Ai cũng có thể viết vào đó. Có khách viết suy nghĩ của mình. Có khách vẽ một bức tranh. Một người đàn ông viết công thức làm bánh. Một người khác viết một bài thơ về người vợ đã khuất của ông ấy.

Bà Lan đọc cuốn sổ vào Chủ Nhật. Bà không trả lời. Bà không cho ai xem. Nhưng bà giữ từng trang.

Một cô gái trẻ có lần hỏi: 'Sao bà lại để cuốn sổ ạ?'

Bà Lan nói: 'Khách của tôi rời nhà trước khi mặt trời mọc. Họ đi làm trước khi thành phố thức. Phần lớn các ngày, không ai nói với họ một câu nào cho đến khi họ tới chỗ làm. Cuốn sổ lắng nghe họ.'

Bà Lan sáu mươi tám tuổi. Một ngày nào đó bà sẽ đóng cửa hàng. Bà chưa nói khi nào. Những cuốn sổ — bây giờ đã có chín cuốn — sẽ đi cùng bà.

questions.prompt: What time does Mrs Lan open her shop?
questions.explanation: 'Every morning at four, before the city wakes up.'

questions.prompt: How long has Mrs Lan been doing this?
questions.explanation: 'She has done this for twenty-three years.'

options: Bus drivers, nurses, cleaners — early workers

questions.prompt: What three things has she always sold?
options: Bread with sweet butter, bread with salty meat, hot tea in small cups

questions.prompt: What did she change last year?
options: She added a free notebook on the corner table
questions.explanation: 'Last year, she put a small notebook on the corner table.'

questions.prompt: What kinds of things have customers written in the notebook?
options: Thoughts, pictures, a recipe for cake, a poem about a late wife
questions.hint: Look at the list in paragraph 4.

questions.prompt: When does Mrs Lan read the notebook?
questions.explanation: 'Mrs Lan reads the notebook on Sundays.'

questions.prompt: Does Mrs Lan respond to what people write?
options: No — she does not respond and does not show it to anyone, but she keeps every page
questions.explanation: 'She does not respond. She does not show it to anyone. But she keeps every page.'

questions.prompt: What does Mrs Lan say the notebook is for?
options: It listens to customers who go to work before anyone talks to them
questions.explanation: 'My customers leave home before the sun… The notebook listens.'
questions.hint: Look at Mrs Lan's reply to the young woman.

questions.prompt: How many notebooks does Mrs Lan now have?
questions.explanation: 'The notebooks — there are now nine — will go with her.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mrs Lan's customers go to work before anyone talks to them. Think of someone you see who works very early or very late. What might it mean for them, even quietly, to find that someone is paying attention?
